text: In Git, branching allows developers to work on new features without affecting the main codebase. A feature branch is a separate copy of the code where changes are made. Once the work is complete, merging integrates these changes back into the main branch. Before merging, teams often use pull requests, which are requests for someone to review and approve the code. This process supports a smooth version control workflow, helping multiple people collaborate without conflicts. Overall, branching keeps development organized and reduces the risk of errors.
